issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, removing the default focus ring without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 376: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector again sets outline: none with only offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for high-contrast mode users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 389-392: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class only adjusts outline-offset but does not restore a visible outline, inheriting outline: none from the global rule. The focus ring remains invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 395: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class sets an outline-color but does not override the outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule, resulting in no visible focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 399: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus state sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 607: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links

issuesFound.fix: Line 722: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input:focus-visible sets outline: none with zero offset, removing the focus ring without replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links

issuesFound.explanation: #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, falling below the 44px recommended target size without sufficient spacing compensation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a (text‑decoration: none)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global anchor selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline on hover or border-bottom), causing links to be distinguished by colour alone which fails WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline:none) and only sets outline‑offset, providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard users lose the default focus ring, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 436: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline without supplying any alternative visual cue (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Users navigating with a keyboard cannot see when a button receives focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm defines min-height: 20px, which is smaller than the WCAG minimum touch target of 44 × 44 px. Users on touch devices may find the button difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 816: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the required 44 px minimum touch target size, reducing tap accuracy for icon‑only buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule uses word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses space between words and harms readability for screen‑reader users and low‑vision readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224: word-spacing: 0;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links
issuesFound.explanation: The global a rule removes underlines (text-decoration:none) without providing any other non‑colour visual distinction, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual cues.
issuesFound.fix: Line 252: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img applies a transform transition (spatial movement) but there is no reduced‑motion override, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1268: .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of .hero-image img changes the transform, creating motion without a reduced‑motion fallback, violating users’ motion‑sensitivity preferences.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1279: .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of .hero-ctas .btn-primary applies a translateY transform (spatial movement) without a reduced‑motion override, affecting users who request reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of .pricing-card--featured applies a translateY and scale transform, creating motion without a reduced‑motion fallback, which can be disorienting for users preferring reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2155: .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation. This prevents users from knowing which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text is displayed to all users, cluttering the visual interface and potentially revealing content intended only for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button has min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with only 6px padding (var(--space-2)), resulting in a touch target of approximately 40px. This is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons have min-width and min-height of 20px, falling below the 24px minimum target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words below normal. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on nav menu items
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation menu links and buttons have min-height: 28px with 6px vertical padding, totaling approximately 40px. This falls below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector removes the outline entirely with no replacement. In high-contrast mode, box-shadow is suppressed, so users receive no focus indicator at all.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible rule suppresses the outline without providing an alternative visual indicator such as box-shadow or border-color change.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the focus outline with no replacement, leaving keyboard users without any indication of focus position.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links set outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring. This blocks keyboard users from identifying the focused element.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion button focus-visible rule removes the outline entirely with no alternative visual indicator, preventing keyboard users from seeing which question is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs in form groups remove the focus outline with no replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to determine which checkbox is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links suppress the focus outline without providing an alternative, leaving keyboard users without focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the focus outline with no visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from tracking focus position.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links set outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. This makes activation difficult for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has height: 28px with 6px vertical padding, resulting in a touch target of approximately 40px, below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, providing only a 28px touch target. This is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons have min-height: 28px with 6px vertical padding, totaling approximately 40px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on pricing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons have min-height: 28px with 6px vertical padding, resulting in a touch target of approximately 40px, below the 44px recommendation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have min-height: 28px with 6px vertical padding, totaling approximately 40px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button includes transform in its transition without a prefers-reduced-motion override, which can cause vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on hero primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button uses background-color: #5588bb with color: #ffffff, resulting in a contrast ratio of 3.54:1. This is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (13.5px font size).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links use color: #5588bb against a white background, providing only 3.54:1 contrast. This is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (10.5px font size).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing toggle active state
issuesFound.explanation: The active pricing toggle button uses background-color: #5588bb with white text, resulting in a 3.54:1 contrast ratio. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use color: #94a3b8 against background-color: #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of 3.04:1. This is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links use color: #94a3b8 against the footer background-color: #555555, providing only 3.04:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer address links use color: #94a3b8 against background-color: #555555, resulting in a 3.04:1 contrast ratio. This is below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom text
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom paragraph text uses color: #64748b against background-color: #555555, resulting in a contrast ratio of 1.66:1. This is below the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer bottom links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer bottom links use color: #94a3b8 against background-color: #555555, providing only 3.04:1 contrast. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) for links in body text. This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color as links are only distinguished by colour.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed on footer navigation with insufficient colour differentiation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links explicitly set text-decoration: none and use the same colour (#94a3b8) as surrounding text (#94a3b8), providing no visual indication that they are links except on hover. This violates WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global selector `:focus-visible` sets `outline: none` and only an offset, providing no visible focus indicator. Keyboard‑only users lose the ability to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: `:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px`, which is smaller than the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 44 px, making the button hard to tap for finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 230: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-3-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, `height: auto`, and `clip: auto`, which does **not** hide content visually while keeping it accessible; instead it leaves the element visible to ATs and sighted users, defeating its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` applies a `transform` transition that moves the image, but there is no corresponding rule inside a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block to disable or simplify the animation, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 150: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }`  
  Line 152: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which compresses space between words and can make text difficult to read, especially for dyslexic users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 80: `body { word-spacing: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Negative letter‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule also sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, reducing space between characters and potentially harming readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 80: `body { letter-spacing: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector removes underlines (`text-decoration: none`) from all links but does not provide another visual cue (e.g., border‑bottom, font‑weight change). Users relying on visual differentiation may not recognize links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function adds the error message element to the field's aria-describedby attribute but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Consequently, screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3520: Insert `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and locks scroll, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening a dropdown menu panel, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes to show the panel, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens an FAQ item by animating the panel height and removing the hidden attribute, but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the WAI-ARIA accordion pattern and prevents screen reader users from knowing which items are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3350: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus management and references it in the close() function, but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it in the open() function. Keyboard users will not be trapped within the mobile navigation when open, allowing focus to escape to background elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830 (in init() after _initialised = true): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` and Line 1850 (in open()): Add `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When file validation fails (size or type check), the function announces the error via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element. Screen readers will not convey the invalid state of the input to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4220: Insert `setAttr(inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `liveRegion.announceAssertive(...)` in the validation failure block

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput validation does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the error message is displayed and announced, but aria-invalid is not set to "true" on the input field. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3950: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the line `_statusEl.textContent = result.message || '';` in the error handling block

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` function adds the “open” CSS classes and shows the drawer, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open()` function (mirroring the `close()` implementation).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance()`, the dropdown panel is shown and CSS classes are added, yet the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`. This leaves the expanded state invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown (and ensure the corresponding `false` is already set on close).

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open(item)` function expands the answer panel and animates it, but it does not update the question button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `"true"`. Users of screen readers cannot detect that the FAQ item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the `open()` routine (the `close()` already sets it to `"false"`).

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form email field validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: In `newsletterForm._onSubmit()`, the email address is validated with `validateField()`, but when the validation fails the code only shows a status message and does not apply `aria-invalid="true"` to the `<input>` element. Assistive technologies therefore cannot convey the error state to users.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` (and remove it on success) or bind the field with `inlineValidator` so that `aria-invalid` is managed automatically.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation open does not set aria‑expanded to true
Dropdown menu open does not set aria‑expanded to true
FAQ accordion open does not set aria‑expanded to true
Newsletter form email field validation does not set aria‑invalid on error

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey the invalid state; without it, users may not realize the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1786: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function updates CSS classes and emits events but never sets the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will still report the menu as collapsed even when it is visually open. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2151 (after `_open = true;`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu changes the visible UI but provides no auditory feedback via a live region. Users navigating with screen readers may not realize the menu has opened or closed, especially when using mouse or touch interactions that do not move focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2353 (end of _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`
  Line 2371 (end of _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown panel removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true". The _closeInstance() function sets it to "false", so the state is only communicated when closing, not when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2349 (after `inst.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ item animates the panel and updates internal state, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true". The init() function sets initial values, and close() sets it to "false", but the open state is never communicated to assistive technologies during runtime toggles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4193 (after `item.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never initialized or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable but never assigns a createFocusTrap() instance to it, and open() never calls activate(). Consequently, focus is not trapped within the mobile menu when opened, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay, and focus is not returned to the trigger button on close.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” routine does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened via click or keyboard, the code reveals the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” routine does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded, the script animates the panel but never sets aria‑expanded on the button, so the expanded state is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers lack an initial aria‑expanded attribute (default false) on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ buttons are created without an explicit aria‑expanded value, violating the requirement that toggle controls convey their default state on load.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The menu’s visibility changes are announced with custom events, but no aria‑live region is updated, so users of assistive technology receive no feedback that the menu opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation (newsletter form)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input is bound with inlineValidator.bind which only adds an error message element; it never adds aria‑invalid, leaving the invalid state invisible to AT.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button lacks an accessible name (no aria‑label or title)
issuesFound.explanation: The switch that toggles monthly/annual billing is operable but provides no name for AT, making its purpose unclear.

fpTitles: Dropdown menu “open” routine does not set aria‑expanded to true on its trigger button
FAQ accordion triggers lack an initial aria‑expanded attribute (default false) on page load
Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on fields that fail validation (newsletter form)

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. This prevents assistive technologies from programmatically identifying the field as having failed validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1680: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function, immediately after the existing `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` line.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to display the mobile navigation drawer and locks scroll, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state when the menu opens, even though close() correctly sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2290: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state announcement for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2450: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `inst.open = true;` in the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function animates the FAQ panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves assistive technologies unaware that the panel is expanded, even though the close() function sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line ~4100: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without activating the focus trap or moving keyboard focus into the drawer. This traps keyboard users at the trigger button and prevents them from accessing the opened menu content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order. The close function correctly deactivates the trap and returns focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2290: Add `_trap.activate();` immediately after the line `_lockScroll();` in the open function to confine focus within the drawer when opened.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button when the navigation drawer is opened
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in the mobile navigation module toggles the drawer visibility and focus trap, but it never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to reflect the expanded state, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after the drawer is made visible.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button when a dropdown is opened
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` function makes the dropdown panel visible but never updates the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute to “true”, so screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true" on the FAQ question button when the answer panel is expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` method expands a FAQ item and animates the panel, but it does not update the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving the state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._updateStatus updates a status element’s text but the element lacks an ARIA live region, so changes (e.g., “0 results found”) are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function sets `textContent` on `_statusEl` without `aria-live`, so users are not informed of search result counts or errors.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite');` (or `assertive` for errors) and ensure the element is not hidden.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm._onSubmit validates the email field but never sets `aria-invalid` on the input when the value is invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The submit handler checks the email with a regex and shows a status message, but it does not mark the `<input>` as invalid via `aria-invalid="true"`, depriving screen‑reader users of error feedback.
issuesFound.fix: When validation fails, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` and clear it on success.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm does not clear `aria-invalid` on the email field after a successful submission, leaving the error state persisted
issuesFound.explanation: After a successful subscription the input’s `aria-invalid` attribute may still be set from a prior failure, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: On success, call `removeAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._debouncedFetch does not announce a loading state via a live region, leaving users unaware that a search request is in progress
issuesFound.explanation: When a user types a query, the UI shows a spinner but no ARIA live announcement is made, so screen‑reader users cannot know that results are being fetched.
issuesFound.fix: Before starting the fetch, set a live region message such as `liveRegion.announce('Searching…');` and clear it when results arrive.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button when a dropdown is opened
faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true" on the FAQ question button when the answer panel is expanded
newsletterForm._onSubmit validates the email field but never sets `aria-invalid` on the input when the value is invalid
newsletterForm does not clear `aria-invalid` on the email field after a successful submission, leaving the error state persisted

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the function deactivates the focus trap and emits a custom event, but it does not announce the state change to screen readers. Users may not be aware that the menu has closed, especially since focus is not explicitly returned to the trigger in this function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1582: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after the emit call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialisation at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function discovers the trigger button and drawer elements but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger when the menu is closed by default on page load. The aria-expanded attribute is never set until the first toggle, violating the requirement that ARIA states must reflect the current state immediately on load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1602: Add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the null checks for _trigger and _drawer.

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs lack keyboard activation
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs use createRovingTabindex for arrow key navigation but rely solely on click events for activation. If the tab elements are not native buttons, they cannot be activated with Enter or Space keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2939: Ensure [data-filter-value] elements are native buttons, or add keydown handler for Enter/Space to trigger _onTabClick.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating the hidden attribute and CSS classes, but they do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger buttons to reflect the current state. Although the register function initialises the attribute to false, it is never updated during toggling.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1687: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance after setting inst.open = true.
  Line 1703: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _closeInstance after setting inst.open = false.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field becomes valid, the markValid function removes the error element reference from aria-describedby but fails to remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". This leaves the field marked as invalid for assistive technologies even after validation passes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3336: Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` after clearing aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the function updates the count badge and emits a custom event, but it does not announce the results to screen readers via a live region. Users may not be aware that the content has changed or how many products are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2898: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the prices update visually and the function emits a custom event, but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not be aware that the pricing context has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3128: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || `Prices switched to ${period} billing`);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle lacks keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle (grid/list) uses delegate to handle clicks on [data-view-mode] elements but does not handle keyboard events. If these are not native buttons, they cannot be activated with Enter or Space keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2912: Add keyboard handler for Enter/Space, or ensure the delegated elements are native buttons.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` in **dropdownMenu** toggle the visibility of a dropdown panel but never set or clear `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1152: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` (add to `_openInstance`)  
    Line 1158: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` (add to `_closeInstance`)

issuesFound.title: Period‑toggle buttons missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: In **pricing.setPeriod** the period buttons are marked with `aria‑selected`, but they act as toggle buttons and should use `aria‑pressed` to convey their pressed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2503: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'true');` (when button matches active period)  
    Line 2509: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', 'false');` (when button does not match)

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger does not set aria‑expanded on open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in **faqAccordion** expands a FAQ item but never updates `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, so screen‑readers cannot detect that the answer is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2104: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: FAQ trigger missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: In **faqRegistry.parse** the trigger button is given `role="button"` but no initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, violating the requirement that toggle controls expose their collapsed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1912: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing aria‑controls
issuesFound.explanation: In **mobileNav.init** the hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) but never receives an `aria‑controls` reference to the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav drawer not hidden from accessibility tree when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` method in **mobileNav** removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` on the drawer element, leaving it perceivable to screen readers even though it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 834: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (add when closing)  
    Line 842: `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');` (add when opening)

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls (already set) – but missing aria‑expanded update (covered in Issue 1). No new issue.
Dropdown menu panels lack `aria‑hidden` handling on close
issuesFound.explanation: In **dropdownMenu._closeInstance** the panel is hidden via CSS but never receives `aria‑hidden="true"`, so the hidden content remains in the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1164: `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (add when closing)  
    Line 1170: `removeAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden');` (add when opening)

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel not given `aria‑hidden` when collapsed (already handled in parse) – skip.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility by adding and removing CSS classes, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu opens or closes, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3580: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the line _open = true; in the open() function.
  Line 3620: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); immediately after the line _open = false; in the close() function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error element from aria-describedby, but it does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the aria-invalid attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after the user has corrected the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3260: In markValid(), after updating aria-describedby, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false'); or add removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate that the menu is initially collapsed. Screen readers will not recognize the button as controlling a collapsible region until it is first activated, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3680: In the init() function, after the line _initialised = true;, add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied in the productFilter.applyFilters() function, the visible product count is updated in the DOM, but there is no announcement to screen readers. Users who cannot see the screen will not know that the filter has been applied or how many results are showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4980: In applyFilters(), after the line _updateCountBadge(totalMatches);, add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing in pricing.setPeriod(), the prices update visually with animation, but the price change is not announced to screen readers. Users will not know that the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5520: In setPeriod(), after the line _updateSavingsMessage(period);, add liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is assigned role="combobox" during initialisation, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially. Screen readers will assume the suggestion list is visible when the page loads, which is incorrect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4720: In init(), after the line const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);, add setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function adds the error message element to the field's aria-describedby, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3240: In markInvalid(), after the line addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);, add setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed in faqAccordion.open() and faqAccordion.close(), the state change is not announced to screen readers. Users may not know whether their action succeeded or whether content is now visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 6080: In open(), after emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });, add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);
  Line 6120: In close(), after emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });, add liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close in _openInstance and _closeInstance, the state change is not announced. While focus moves to the menu or back to the trigger, the context change (menu opened/closed) should be announced for clarity.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3780: In _openInstance(), after emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });, add liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 3810: In _closeInstance(), after emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });, add liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: While mobileNav.open() announces "Navigation menu opened" via liveRegion.announce(), the close() function does not announce the closure. Users will not know that the menu has closed, especially if focus did not move to a specific element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3620: In close(), before the line emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function dynamically updates the product grid and count badge when filters are applied, but it does not announce the filter results (such as the number of products shown or the active filter category) to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4580: Add `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? 'No products match the selected filters' : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);` immediately after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute on the navigation toggle button when the module initializes. Since the menu is closed by default, the trigger button should have aria-expanded="false" to indicate the initial state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2860: Add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The setPeriod function in the pricing module updates price displays when users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, but it does not announce the billing period change or the new prices to screen readers. Users may not be aware that the pricing information has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5220: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing' : STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing');` immediately after the plan cards are updated

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates function in the productFilter module updates the visual active state of filter tab buttons but does not set the aria-pressed attribute. Since these tabs act as toggle buttons that remain pressed when active, aria-pressed is required to communicate the active state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4656: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` inside the forEach loop

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not establish an aria-controls relationship between the navigation toggle button and the drawer element it controls. This prevents screen readers from identifying which element is controlled by the toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2862: Add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id || srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));` immediately after the aria-expanded initialization

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function does not announce the menu closure to screen readers, unlike the open() function which announces "Navigation menu opened". Users may not be aware that the menu has closed, especially if focus is not explicitly moved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2840: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }` immediately before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in the srUtils module adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2422: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid cleanup
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description association but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute from the field. This leaves stale invalid state information on fields that have been corrected, causing screen readers to still report them as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2440: Add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` immediately after the existing code that updates aria-describedby

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function toggles the drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside open() after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() hides the drawer without resetting aria‑expanded to false on the trigger button, so the accessibility state remains stale.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); inside close() when the drawer is hidden.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (_trigger) has no aria‑controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')); during init().

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS but not aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (_drawer) is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but its aria‑hidden state is never updated, so it remains perceivable to assistive technologies when hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In open() call removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden'); and in close() call setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() does not use liveRegion.announce to inform users that the navigation menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding the drawer, add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosedAnnouncement || 'Navigation menu closed');.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger click does not update aria‑expanded on button
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._onTriggerClick() toggles the dropdown but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the trigger button, leaving the expanded state undisclosed.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); and after closing, setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown does not provide an aria‑live announcement, so users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance() and _closeInstance() add liveRegion.announce messages such as “Menu opened” / “Menu closed”.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel open does not set aria‑expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands an answer panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated button, breaking the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening and setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when closing.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing a FAQ item does not trigger an aria‑live announcement, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix: After open(item) call liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} – answer expanded`); and after close(item) announce `answer collapsed`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle does not announce changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod() updates the displayed prices but does not announce the billing‑period change, leaving users unaware of the new pricing.
issuesFound.fix: After updating prices, add liveRegion.announce(`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`);.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button lacks aria‑controls to associate with price display region
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle element (_toggleEl) does not have an aria‑controls attribute pointing to the region that updates prices, breaking the relationship for assistive tech.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_priceDisplayContainer, 'price-display')); where _priceDisplayContainer is the element containing the price values.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle button does not set aria‑expanded or aria‑pressed to reflect state
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle switch changes visual state but does not expose its state via aria‑expanded or aria‑pressed, making its status invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In _ syncToggleState() or _ onToggleClick(), set setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); or setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-expanded', String(isYear));.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class but not removed from accessibility tree when closed (duplicate of Issue 4) – already covered.
Dropdown menu panels are hidden using hidden attribute (good) but the trigger button’s aria‑expanded is not synchronized (already covered in Issue 6).

Mobile navigation drawer hidden via CSS class but not removed from accessibility tree when closed (duplicate of Issue 4) – already covered.
Dropdown menu panels are hidden using hidden attribute (good) but the trigger button’s aria‑expanded is not synchronized (already covered in Issue 6).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer toggle updates CSS classes and body state but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the drawer is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1248: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside the open() function after `_open = true;`
    Line 1268: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside the close() function after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content but does not announce the state change or the answer content to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2678: Inside open(), after `_lastOpenItem = item;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question));`
    Line 2700: Inside close(), after `if (_lastOpenItem === item) _lastOpenItem = null;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update their aria-expanded state when panels open or close, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1388: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _openInstance() after `inst.open = true;`
    Line 1402: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _closeInstance() after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the page content significantly but does not announce the state change to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1248: Inside open(), after `_lockScroll();`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);`
    Line 1268: Inside close(), after `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: pricing init missing aria-checked initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period switch element receives role="switch" but does not have aria-checked initialized to reflect the default period state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2440: After `if (!_toggleEl.getAttribute('role')) _toggleEl.setAttribute('role', 'switch');`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function removes the hidden attribute and adds CSS classes but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (though the close function sets it to false).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2678: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside the open() function after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal action missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users click to remove a filter chip, the product grid updates but the removal action and updated result count are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2220: At the end of _onChipClick() function (after the switch statement and render() call), add `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but its aria-expanded state is never updated when the suggestion list opens or closes, preventing screen readers from announcing the list visibility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1982: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` inside _open() after `_searchState.open = true;`
    Line 1990: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` inside _close() after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid/markValid missing aria-invalid updates
issuesFound.explanation: Form validation functions update aria-describedby to link error messages but never set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields or aria-invalid="false" on valid fields, preventing assistive technologies from identifying invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1020: Inside markInvalid(), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after addDescribedBy()
    Line 1028: Inside markValid(), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` before the if block checking errorEl

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button does not have aria-expanded="false" set on page load when the drawer is closed, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered with aria-haspopup and aria-controls but lack aria-expanded="false" initialization when the panel is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1480: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch mount missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is wired with aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded="false" initialization when the suggestion list is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1910: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without activating a focus trap, allowing keyboard users to tab to background content behind the drawer. The _trap variable is declared but never initialized.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1320: After `_initialised = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
    Line 1248: Inside open(), after `_lockScroll();`, add `_trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria-controls referencing the drawer element, preventing screen readers from identifying which element is controlled by the button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (!_drawer.id) _drawer.id = 'mobile-nav-drawer'; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The internal function `_openInstance(inst)` shows a dropdown panel but never sets `aria‑expanded="true"` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance(inst, returnFocus)` hides the dropdown panel but never resets `aria‑expanded` on the trigger button, so assistive technologies still think the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons lack initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is registered the script adds `aria-haspopup` but does **not** set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger, violating the requirement that the attribute be present on page load.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons do not reflect state with aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons are visually toggled with the `active` class, but the required ARIA state `aria-pressed` is never updated, so assistive technology cannot tell which period is selected.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer does not set aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the off‑canvas drawer only removes CSS classes; it never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the drawer element, leaving it in the accessibility tree while invisible.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated, but no live‑region announcement is made, so users of assistive technology are unaware that the result set changed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panel opening is not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened the script updates ARIA attributes but does not provide an audible announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the newly revealed answer.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly/quarterly/annual pricing updates the UI but does not send a live‑region message, so users cannot hear which billing period is now active.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown emits custom events but does not provide a live‑region announcement, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback when a menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile menu updates CSS classes but does not announce the state change, leaving assistive‑technology users uninformed.
