issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is marked invalid, the function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in markInvalid, after addDescribedBy): 
  `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance/_closeInstance missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden, but the trigger button's aria-expanded state is not synchronized. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance): Add after `inst.open = true;`:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line (in _closeInstance): Add after `inst.open = false;`:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price changes visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware of the update.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in setPeriod, after `_syncToggleState(period);`):
  `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true" (though the close function sets it to "false"). This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open function, after `item.open = true;`): 
  `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls but no confirmation is announced to screen readers that the action completed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _onClick, after scrollToY call):
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (monthly/annual) function as toggle buttons but do not update aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _syncToggleState, inside forEach): Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with:
  `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open/_close missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox trigger for the suggestion list, but aria-expanded is never updated when the suggestion panel opens or closes, violating the combobox pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _open): Add after `_searchState.open = true;`:
  `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line (in _close): Add after `_searchState.open = false;`:
  `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips missing aria-live announcement on removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a filter chip is removed, the filter is cleared but no announcement confirms the removal or the updated results to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _onChipClick, end of function):
  `liveRegion.announce(\`Removed \${label} filter\`);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared/validated, the function removes error associations but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute, leaving stale invalid state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in markValid, end of function): 
  `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input element lacks aria-expanded="false" on initialization, which is required for combobox pattern compliance.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in init, after `_initialised = true;`):
  `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial DOM state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in init, after `_initialised = true;`):
  `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when registered, leaving the initial state undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in register, after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`):
  `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause toggle button is initialized without aria-pressed, and the attribute is only set when toggled. The initial state is not communicated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in init, after play button event binding):
  `setAttr(_playBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField zone missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The upload zone has role="button" but lacks aria-controls to associate it with the file list container, making the relationship unclear to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in bind, after setting role and tabindex):
  `setAttr(zoneEl, ARIA.CONTROLS, listEl.id);`

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When email validation fails, the field is focused but aria-invalid is not set to "true", so screen readers don't announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _onSubmit, after `inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl);` and before focus):
  `setAttr(_emailEl, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`
  And on success/valid: `setAttr(_emailEl, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: contactForm validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: While validating fields, the code relies on inlineValidator which doesn't set aria-invalid. Invalid fields need explicit aria-invalid="true".
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _validateAll, inside forEach after getting result):
  Add `if (!result.valid) setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); else setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard validation missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Step validation checks fields but doesn't ensure aria-invalid is set on invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _validateCurrentStep, inside forEach after validation):
  Add `if (result && !result.valid) setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); else setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the navigation drawer but never references the drawer via `aria-controls`. Without this relationship screen readers cannot locate the controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger button missing `aria-pressed` (two‑state toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The button acts as a binary toggle (open / closed) but never reflects its state with `aria-pressed`, which is required for toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button does not update `aria-expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened the associated button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `true`, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the panel’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger button missing initial `aria-expanded="false"`
issuesFound.explanation: The button is given `aria-haspopup` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` value, so its collapsed state is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: Search input (combobox) does not update `aria-expanded` when suggestions open
issuesFound.explanation: The search box acts as a combobox; when the suggestion list becomes visible the component must set `aria-expanded="true"`. The code never does this, breaking the ARIA combobox pattern.

issuesFound.title: Search input missing initial `aria-expanded="false"`
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox input is given `role="combobox"` and `aria-haspopup="listbox"` but lacks an explicit `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on load, violating the ARIA combobox specification.
issuesFound.fix:
```javascript
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');   // ← add during init
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button does not update `aria-expanded` on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded the associated button’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `true`, and similarly never set to `false` on close, preventing AT users from perceiving the state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger button missing initial `aria-expanded="false"`
issuesFound.explanation: The button is given `aria-controls` but never receives an initial `aria-expanded="false"` value, so its collapsed state is not announced.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria-invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the code calls `srUtils.markInvalid`, which only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"`. WCAG 4.1.2 requires the ARIA invalid state to be reflected.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not clear `aria-invalid` on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a previously‑invalid field becomes valid, the code removes the descriptive error but does not remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`, leaving the invalid state lingering for AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The UI announces navigation opening/closing through `STRINGS.announce.navOpen` / `navClosed`, but these strings are never sent to the shared live region, so screen‑reader users receive no feedback.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown emits custom events but never announces the state change to AT. Users need an audible cue that a menu has opened or closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion fires custom events but does not provide an audible status update, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the expanded/collapsed state.

issuesFound.title: Search suggestions panel open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the suggestion list appears or disappears the component does not announce the change, so AT users cannot know that suggestions are available.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid omits aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so screen-reader users are not informed of the validation error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850: setAttr(control, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the hamburger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true". Screen-reader users cannot perceive that the menu is now expanded and interactive.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1172: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open omits focus-trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the off-canvas menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer; keyboard users can Tab out of the menu and into the background page, breaking modal accessibility expectations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1172: if (!_trap) _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close omits aria-expanded update on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never reset to "false". Assistive technologies continue to announce the menu as expanded even though it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown panel does not update the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute to "true", violating the disclosure pattern and leaving screen-reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1290: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters omits live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Filtering products updates the visible card set and count badge but never announces the results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen-reader users, who must manually discover the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates omits aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: Category filter tab buttons visually indicate the active filter but do not set aria-pressed, preventing screen-reader users from knowing which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a dropdown panel does not reset the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute to "false", causing assistive technologies to incorrectly report the menu as still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1303: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod omits live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods dynamically updates prices but does not announce the change (e.g., "Prices switched to annual billing") via a live region, so screen-reader users miss the pricing update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState omits aria-pressed on billing buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing-period toggle buttons (Monthly/Annual) are styled as active/inactive but never update aria-pressed, so screen-reader users cannot identify which billing mode is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid omits aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When a field is cleared or marked valid, the function removes the error description but leaves aria-invalid="true" (or undefined), causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 870: setAttr(control, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init omits aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: On page load the mobile-nav trigger button lacks aria-expanded="false", so screen-reader users are unaware that a collapsible menu is present until they interact with it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1210: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register omits aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" even though their panels are hidden by default, leaving assistive technologies without state context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open omits aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion listbox opens, the combobox input does not set aria-expanded="true", violating the ARIA combobox pattern and hiding the popup state from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2600: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open omits live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding an FAQ item reveals new content without announcing the expansion or the answer text to screen-reader users, who may not realize the panel has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close omits live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing an FAQ item does not announce the collapsed state, leaving screen-reader users uncertain whether the action succeeded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2870: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open omits live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen-reader users, who may not realize the menu is now available for interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1172: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close omits live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced, so screen-reader users may attempt to navigate within a menu they believe is still open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `register()` function for dropdown menus adds `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls` to the trigger but never sets or updates `aria‑expanded` when the panel is opened or closed. Screen‑reader users cannot know the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix: In the `_openInstance()` function add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance()` set it to `'false'`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is initialized the trigger button has no `aria‑expanded` attribute, so its default state is ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: In `register()` set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` after adding `aria-haspopup`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded true
issuesFound.explanation: In `faqAccordion.open()` the panel is shown but the trigger’s `aria‑expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"`. Users of assistive technology cannot detect that the answer is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` at the start of the `open()` function.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ items are parsed without initializing `aria‑expanded` on the trigger, leaving the initial collapsed state undefined for AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse()` set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when creating each item.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the navigation drawer but never receives an `aria-controls` reference to the drawer’s ID, breaking the relationship for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After locating `_drawer`, generate an ID if needed (`srUtils.ensureId(_drawer)`) and call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', drawerId)` during `mobileNav.init()`.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription email field does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: Validation of the email input uses `validateField` and shows a status message, but never marks the `<input>` with `aria-invalid="true"` when the address is invalid, leaving the error invisible to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: After a failed validation, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; on success, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter result count updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateCountBadge()` updates the visible count badge but does not send an announcement to a live region, so users of screen readers are unaware when the number of matching products changes.
issuesFound.fix: After setting the badge text, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)` (or similar) to a polite live region.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer hidden state not reflected with aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile drawer is closed (`close()`), the element is hidden by removing CSS classes but never receives `aria-hidden="true"`. The drawer remains in the accessibility tree, causing focus traps to fail for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: In `close()` add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `open()` remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing the mobile navigation menu triggers visual changes and focus management but does not announce the state change to a screen‑reader, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: After `open()` call `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu opened')`; after `close()` call `liveRegion.announce('Navigation menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Selecting a different billing period updates prices but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the price change.
issuesFound.fix: At the end of `setPeriod()` add `liveRegion.announce(`Billing period set to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}`)`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panel visibility changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Panels are shown/hidden with CSS classes but no live‑region update occurs, so AT users cannot perceive the menu state change.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')`; after closing, `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown disclosure trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button manages an open/close state (isOpen) and has aria-controls linking to the submenu, but lacks aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot announce whether the submenu is expanded or collapsed, leaving users unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons implement a single-select toggle pattern (activeFilter state) but do not communicate the selected state to assistive technology. Users cannot identify which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1376: <button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not visually visible (isVisible === false) remain in the accessibility tree without aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce content from hidden slides. This creates a confusing experience where users hear content they cannot see.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1558: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that opens the dropdown menu does not have an aria-expanded attribute that reflects the isOpen state. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 312: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion button that toggles the FAQ answer panel does not expose its expanded state via aria-expanded, leaving AT users unaware of the panel’s visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1248: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The email <TextInput> receives an “invalid” prop but the TextInput component does not map this to aria‑invalid, so validation errors are not announced to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: <TextInput id="newsletter-email" type="email" name="email" placeholder="you@company.com" autoComplete="email" value={email.state.value} onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={email.onBlur} required invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input’s aria-describedby points to “newsletter-email-error”, but no element with that id exists, so screen readers receive a dangling reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1025: replace aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} with aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email' : undefined} (or add an element with id="newsletter-email-error").

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The name <TextInput> receives an “invalid” prop but TextInput does not set aria‑invalid, so error state is not conveyed to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1382: <TextInput id="contact-name" name="name" type="text" required aria-invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error} aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The email <TextInput> does not set aria‑invalid when in error state, preventing AT from announcing validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1405: <TextInput id="contact-email" name="email" type="email" required aria-invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error} aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The subject <TextInput> does not convey its error state via aria‑invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1428: <TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The message <Textarea> does not set aria‑invalid when validation fails, so AT cannot announce the error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1452: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={['contact-message-counter', message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} />

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` prop to indicate error states but fails to map it to the `aria-invalid` attribute. Screen readers cannot announce when the field contains invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 946: Add `aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined}` to the input element before the `{...rest}` spread.

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent accordion trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger button toggles the visibility of the answer panel but does not communicate its expanded/collapsed state to assistive technology via aria-expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1924: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element.

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing toggle buttons indicate selection visually but do not communicate the pressed state programmatically via aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1725: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} to the first button. Line 1733: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} to the second button.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when in error state, but the FormGroup component is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: Add errorId="newsletter-email-error" to the FormGroup component props.

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs toggle button missing aria-pressed (Reporting)
issuesFound.explanation: The filter button acts as a toggle for the "Reporting" category. It visually indicates the active state via CSS class but does not programmatically communicate the pressed state to assistive technology using aria-pressed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1260: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} to the button element: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide 2 missing aria-hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonial slide is visually hidden when not active but remains in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users can navigate to content that is not visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1460: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the slide div: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={\`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}\`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide 3 missing aria-hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonial slide is visually hidden when not active but remains in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users can navigate to content that is not visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1460: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the slide div: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={\`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}\`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel slide 4 missing aria-hidden when not visible
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonial slide is visually hidden when not active but remains in the accessibility tree. Screen reader users can navigate to content that is not visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1460: Add aria-hidden={!isVisible} to the slide div: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={\`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}\`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens and closes a navigation submenu does not expose its open/closed state to assistive technology, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is visible.

issuesFound.title: FAQ item button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion button that shows/hides an FAQ answer does not convey its expanded state, preventing screen‑reader users from knowing whether the answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: TextInput does not set aria‑invalid when invalid prop true
issuesFound.explanation: The custom <TextInput> component accepts an invalid prop but never reflects it as aria‑invalid, so AT cannot detect an error state.

issuesFound.title: TextInput does not set aria‑invalid when invalid prop true (ContactForm – name)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 3 for the “Full name” field.
issuesFound.fix: add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the <TextInput> props (see Issue 3 for exact placement).

issuesFound.title: TextInput does not set aria‑invalid when invalid prop true (ContactForm – email)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 3 for the “Email address” field.
issuesFound.fix: add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the <TextInput> props.

issuesFound.title: TextInput does not set aria‑invalid when invalid prop true (ContactForm – subject)
issuesFound.explanation: Same as Issue 3 for the “Subject” field.
issuesFound.fix: add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the <TextInput> props.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email field aria‑describedby points to non‑existent ID
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter references newsletter-email-error via aria-describedby, but no element with that ID is rendered, so screen readers cannot locate the error message.
issuesFound.fix:
give the FormGroup the matching errorId prop and pass it to ErrorMessage:  
  Line 1479:    <FormGroup  
  Line 1480:     label="Email address"  
  Line 1481:     htmlFor="newsletter-email"  
  Line 1482:     errorId="newsletter-email-error"  ← add  
  Line 1483:     error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}  
  …  
  Then ensure <TextInput> uses the same ID:  
  Line 1495:     aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the input contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line TextInput: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the visibility of mobile navigation sub-sections using the isExpanded state but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology users cannot perceive whether the section content is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line MobileNav: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button acts as a toggle for the billing period selection but lacks aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify this as a toggle button or determine if it is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line BillingToggle: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button acts as a toggle for the billing period selection but lacks aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot identify this as a toggle button or determine if it is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line BillingToggle: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message element via aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but the FormGroup component does not receive the errorId prop, so the ErrorMessage element lacks this id. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line NewsletterForm: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states but does not map it to the aria-invalid attribute. Screen reader users cannot perceive when the textarea contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line Textarea: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop for accessible naming, but the previous slide button does not provide one. Screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of this icon-only button.
issuesFound.fix: Line TestimonialsCarousel: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The HeroSection section element lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby. Since multiple section landmarks exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this region from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line HeroSection: <section aria-labelledby="hero-heading" className="hero-section">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop for accessible naming, but the next slide button does not provide one. Screen reader users cannot identify the purpose of this icon-only button.
issuesFound.fix: Line TestimonialsCarousel: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the desktop navigation is visually indicated with a CSS class but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line DesktopNav: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in the mobile navigation is visually indicated with a CSS class but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line MobileNav: <a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>

issuesFound.title: StatsBar landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The StatsBar section element lacks aria-label or aria-labelledby. Since multiple section landmarks exist, screen reader users cannot distinguish this statistics region from others.
issuesFound.fix: Line StatsBar: <section aria-labelledby="stats-heading" className="stats-bar">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The PlanCard article element contains a heading with id={headingId} but lacks aria-labelledby to expose that heading as the accessible name. Screen reader users cannot identify the specific pricing plan when navigating by article landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line PlanCard: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks nav element lacks an aria-label. Since multiple nav elements exist on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish the skip navigation from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line SkipLinks: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: HeroSection trust badges group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The trust badges container groups related certification badges but lacks an aria-label. Screen reader users encounter the items without context for what the group represents.
issuesFound.fix: Line HeroSection: <div className="hero-section__trust" aria-label="Trust badges and certifications">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav footer CTAs group missing aria-label
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation footer contains call-to-action buttons but lacks an aria-label to identify the group purpose. Screen reader users may not understand the context of these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line MobileNav: <div className="mobile-nav__footer" aria-label="Account actions">

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the overlay backdrop instead of the dialog element itself. This may cause screen readers to incorrectly identify the modal boundaries and focus scope.
issuesFound.fix: Line Modal: Move aria-modal="true" to the inner div: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel has role="dialog" but lacks aria-modal="true", which is required to indicate that the dialog is modal and interaction with the rest of the page is restricted while open.
issuesFound.fix: Line MobileNav: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.fix: Line ContactForm: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a sub-menu but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: Required field marker not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker for required fields is not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen readers to announce "star" in addition to the visually hidden "(required)" text, resulting in redundant or confusing announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line: *

issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type="button" aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may attempt to describe the spinner element separately from the loading label text.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion trigger button missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation lacks an accessible name. With multiple nav elements present, users navigating by landmarks cannot identify this as the skip navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <nav aria-label="Skip links">

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile navigation toggle missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input missing associated <label>
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> relies only on aria-label; without a <label> element screen‑reader users may miss the field’s purpose in some contexts.
issuesFound.fix: Add a <label htmlFor="newsletter-email">Email address</label> before the <TextInput> or ensure FormGroup renders the label with htmlFor.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input missing aria-invalid when invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component does not forward the “invalid” prop to an aria‑invalid attribute, so AT users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: In TextInput component, add  aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}  to the <input>.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Full name” input missing associated <label>
issuesFound.explanation: The name field is rendered inside FormGroup without a proper <label htmlFor>, breaking the label‑control relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Modify FormGroup to render  <label htmlFor={htmlFor}>…</label  or pass a correctly associated label element.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Full name” input missing aria-invalid when invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput does not expose its error state via aria-invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Add  aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}  to the TextInput component (as in Issue 24).

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Email address” input missing associated <label>
issuesFound.explanation: Same label association issue as Issue 25 for the email field.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Email address” input missing aria-invalid when invalid

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Subject” input missing associated <label>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Subject” input missing aria-invalid when invalid

issuesFound.fix: Add  aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}  to the TextInput component.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Message” textarea missing associated <label>
issuesFound.explanation: The textarea is not linked to a <label>, breaking accessibility.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm “Message” textarea missing aria-invalid when invalid
issuesFound.explanation: Missing aria-invalid attribute on the textarea.
issuesFound.fix: Add  aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined}  to the Textarea component.

issuesFound.title: Required‑field marker missing aria-hidden (Full name)

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a visible sub-menu using the isOpen state, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: StarRating decorative stars not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The individual star symbols are decorative content inside a container that already provides an aria-label with the full rating text, but the stars lack aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the star characters redundantly.
issuesFound.fix: Line: {filled ? '★' : '☆'}

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The message textarea references an error message ID 'contact-message-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" error={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon and no visible text or aria-label attribute, despite IconButton requiring an aria-label prop. Screen reader users encounter an unlabeled button.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" aria-label={LABELS[theme]}>

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element containing a heading with a generated ID, but lacks aria-labelledby to associate the article with its heading. Screen reader users navigating by landmark or article cannot identify which plan the card represents.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button contains only a chevron character with no text content or aria-label, and the IconButton component requires an aria-label prop. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev" aria-label="Previous testimonial">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button contains only a chevron character with no text content or aria-label, and the IconButton component requires an aria-label prop. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <IconButton aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next" aria-label="Next testimonial">›</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it renders a spinner but does not set aria-busy to communicate the loading state to assistive technology. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is busy processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The name input references an error message ID 'contact-name-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" error={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID 'contact-email-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input references an error message ID 'contact-subject-error' via aria-describedby when in error state, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. Screen readers cannot announce the error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" error={subject.state.touched ? subject.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav nav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element lacks an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (DesktopNav, MobileNav, FooterNavGroup, etc.), screen reader users cannot distinguish between them when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Main navigation">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav internal nav landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation element inside the mobile panel lacks an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish between them when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: Section component ignores labelledby prop
issuesFound.explanation: The Section component accepts a labelledby prop but does not apply it to the rendered element via aria-labelledby, preventing sections from being properly labeled when the prop is provided.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <Tag id={id} aria-labelledby={labelledby} className={['page-section', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section contains a heading with id="hero-heading" but lacks aria-labelledby to associate the section with its heading. Screen reader users navigating by landmark cannot identify the section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The statistics section contains a heading with id="stats-heading" but lacks aria-labelledby to associate the section with its heading. Screen reader users navigating by landmark cannot identify the section's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <section className="stats-bar" aria-labelledby="stats-heading">

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron symbol (▲/▼) is decorative content indicating visual state, but lacks aria-hidden. Screen readers may announce the triangle character, which is redundant to the aria-expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav backdrop div with onClick missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop element has an onClick handler to close the navigation but is a div without role="button", tabIndex, or keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot focus or activate the backdrop to close the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" onClick={onClose} role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close navigation menu" onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }} />

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay div with onClick missing button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The overlay element has an onClick handler to close the modal but is a div without role="button", tabIndex, or keyboard event handlers. Keyboard users cannot focus or activate the overlay to close the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose} role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close dialog" onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); onClose(); } }}>

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker not hidden from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk marker indicating a required field is not hidden from assistive technology, causing screen readers to announce "star" in addition to the "(required)" text provided by VisuallyHidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: *

issuesFound.title: Disclosure toggle missing aria-expanded on mobile navigation panel toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav toggle button controls the off‑canvas panel but lacks aria-expanded, preventing AT from announcing the panel’s open/closed state. Affects keyboard‑only and screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 642: `<button id={toggleId} ref={menuToggleRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-label={isOpen ? 'Close navigation menu' : 'Open navigation menu'} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="mobile-nav-toggle" onClick={isOpen ? close : open}>`

issuesFound.title: Disclosure toggle missing aria-expanded on mobile submenu button
issuesFound.explanation: Each mobile submenu button (used to expand a dropdown list) does not have aria-expanded bound to its open state, so users of assistive technology cannot tell whether the submenu items are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: Disclosure toggle missing aria-expanded on FAQ item button
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion button toggles the answer panel but does not include aria-expanded, leaving AT users unaware of the panel’s state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1062: `<button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>`

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (ThemeToggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an <IconButton> that contains only an emoji icon and provides no aria-label, aria-labelledby, or title, so the button is invisible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 730: `<IconButton aria-label={theme === 'light' ? 'Switch to dark theme' : theme === 'dark' ? 'Switch to system theme' : 'Switch to light theme'} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">`

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (mobile nav close)
issuesFound.explanation: The close button inside the mobile navigation panel is an <IconButton> with only a “✕” character and no accessible name, making it unreadable to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Line 688: `<IconButton aria-label="Close mobile navigation" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (carousel previous control)
issuesFound.explanation: The previous‑slide control in the testimonials carousel is an <IconButton> that contains only a “‹” character and lacks an accessible name, so screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Icon‑only button missing accessible name (carousel next control)
issuesFound.explanation: The next‑slide control in the testimonials carousel is an <IconButton> with only a “›” character and no accessible name, preventing AT users from navigating the carousel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1156: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: Form fields missing associated <label> (FormGroup does not link label to input)
issuesFound.explanation:
<FormGroup> renders a <label> without an htmlFor attribute, so none of the inputs are programmatically associated with their visible labels. This blocks screen‑reader users from understanding the purpose of each field.  
  Fix (example for NewsletterForm):  
  Line 842: `<label htmlFor="newsletter-email" className="form-label">Email address</label>`  
  (Apply the same htmlFor pattern to every <FormGroup> instance.)

issuesFound.title: Form fields missing aria-invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation:
<TextInput> receives an invalid prop but does not forward aria-invalid to the underlying <input>, so AT cannot detect validation errors.  
  Fix (example for NewsletterForm email input):  
  Line 860: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} … />`  
  (Add the same aria-invalid prop handling to the TextInput component.)

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby reference to error message (NewsletterForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The email <TextInput> sets aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with that id exists, so the error cannot be announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line 860: Change aria-describedby to reference the actual ErrorMessage id, e.g., `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? "newsletter-email-error" : undefined}` and ensure an <ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" …/> is rendered.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby reference to error message (ContactForm – name)
issuesFound.explanation: The name input points to contact-name-error which is never rendered, breaking the error association.
issuesFound.fix: Line 916: Ensure an <ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" …/> is rendered inside the <FormGroup> or remove the aria-describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby reference to error message (ContactForm – email)
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references contact-email-error that does not exist.
issuesFound.fix: Render an <ErrorMessage id="contact-email-error" …/> or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby reference to error message (ContactForm – subject)
issuesFound.explanation: The subject input references contact-subject-error which is never rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Add an <ErrorMessage id="contact-subject-error" …/> or drop the reference.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby reference to error message (ContactForm – message)
issuesFound.explanation: The message <textarea> includes contact-message-error in its aria-describedby list, but no such element is ever rendered.
issuesFound.fix: Render an <ErrorMessage id="contact-message-error" …/> or remove the reference.

issuesFound.title: Required‑field visual marker not hidden from AT (ContactForm)
issuesFound.explanation: The asterisk  * is announced by screen readers, causing unnecessary verbosity. Fix (example for name field): Line 904: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy (state not communicated) – NewsletterForm submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> uses the loading prop (which the component does not recognize) and does not set aria-busy, so AT cannot know the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy (state not communicated) – ContactForm submit button
issuesFound.explanation: Same issue as above for the contact form submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1080: `<Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting}>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current (DesktopNav – Products)
issuesFound.explanation: The “Products” link has className="nav-link--current" but no aria-current="page", so AT cannot identify the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 332: `{item.label}`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current (MobileNav – current link)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation link for the current page uses className="mobile-nav__link--current" without aria-current, preventing AT from recognizing the active page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 587: `{item.label}`

issuesFound.title: Repeated landmark <section> missing accessible name (HeroSection)
issuesFound.explanation: The hero <section> has no aria-labelledby or aria-label, so screen‑reader users cannot jump directly to this major page region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: `<section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">`

issuesFound.title: Repeated <nav> missing accessible name (mobile navigation panel)
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> inside MobileNav has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it indistinguishable from the main navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: `<nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: Repeated <nav> missing accessible name (skip‑links navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> that contains the skip‑links lacks an accessible name, which can confuse AT users when multiple <nav> landmarks exist.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed (state not communicated) – “Testing”

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed (state not communicated) – “Reporting”
issuesFound.fix: Line 1026: `<button type="button" className="filter-tab-btn" aria-pressed="false" …>Reporting</button>`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1190: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className="billing-toggle__btn billing-toggle__btn--active" aria-pressed="true" …>Monthly</button>`
